valueString.value: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

description.value: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for radiation monitoring and crew personal dosimetry. Captures crew radiation exposure data for occupational exposure documentation and health risk assessment.

purpose.value: To monitor and document crew exposure to radiation and to maintain crew exposures 'as low as reasonably achievable'. To perform risk assessment.

copyright.value: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

valueString.value: MEDB 3.1 Radiation Monitoring/Crew Personal Dosimetry
Medical Requirements
SSP 50260 ISS Medical Operations Requirement Document; SSP 50667 Med Volume B, Section 3.1
To monitor and document crew exposure to radiation and to maintain crew exposures 'as low as reasonably achievable'. To perform risk assessment.
Radiation exposure

Record of radiation doses used to document occupational exposure. Doses from each mission and accumulated doses, which shall be used for health risk assessment, are to be recorded in crewmembers' medical records. All U.S. crewmembers' medical records will be updated based on CPD data.
All flights
Every ISS Increment

text.value: Number and Type of Crew Members Required

valueString.value: ISS crewmembers (Primary and backup will be trained)
Training will be covered in the following Radiation lesson (ISS): EHS Radiation Operations.
EHS Radiation Operations
Conference Room
No crew activities

When transporting hardware to launch location, hardware should not be x-rayed or stowed/shipped with radioactive material. Dosimeters should be transported inside lead-lined bags. If X-ray is necessary for security, the dosimeters must be kept inside the lead-lined bags and the number of X-rays should be recorded.
If a launch delay of more than 90 days occurs, Space Radiation Analysis Group (SRAG) will decide whether to refurbish, replace or take no action.

The Crew Passive Dosimeters (CPDs) are preintegrated into the crewed launch vehicle and then distributed to the appropriate crewmembers post launch. Each crewmember is required to carry his/her CPD continuously, including during EVAs. Prior to descent, crewmembers are to wear or stow the CPDs to be recovered at landing.
Crew Passive Dosimeter
Continuous monitoring
Worn continuously during the mission, including ascent and EVAs
All U.S. crewmembers, CSA and JAXA crewmembers by agreement

Resupply Requirements: At launch each crewmember will be supplied with a crew passive dosimeter (CPD) for continuous wear. Longer duration crew (greater than 180 days) may receive mid-mission CPDs to exchange with their beginning of mission CPD to assess mid-mission exposures.
Returning Crewmembers will have their dosimeters returned via flight surgeons.

Upon the return of hardware, hardware should not be x-rayed or stowed/shipped with radioactive material. Dosimeters should be transported inside lead-lined bags. If X-ray is necessary for security, the dosimeters must be kept inside the lead-lined bags and the number of X-rays should be recorded.
No extra time (included as part of Med Ops overall debrief)
Crewmembers/Radiation Team
